symptoms.symptom_name: Carpopedal spasm (hands/feet clawing) reported and video-observable

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Carpopedal spasm is a hallmark of neuromuscular hyperexcitability from low ionised calcium.
Can also cause carpopedal spasm via respiratory alkalosis, but does not fit the wider picture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Perioral numbness and tingling preceding spasm

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Perioral paraesthesia is an early and characteristic feature of acute hypocalcaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive proximal flaccid weakness (unable to lift arms / walk)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mixed cation depletion (hypokalaemia contributing) produces proximal weakness alongside the calcium-driven tetany.
Ascending weakness is a mimic to keep in mind, but the tetany and sensory spasm argue against it.
CNS involvement signals a severe, unstable derangement and raises seizure risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent prolonged GI illness with completed broad-spectrum antibiotic course

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prolonged GI losses deplete calcium, magnesium and potassium — the plausible cause of the multi-cation depletion.
Clinician-elicited signs of latent/overt tetany — sealed clinician examination; the AI infers hyperexcitability from the reported spasm and numbness, not an elicited sign.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum calcium / magnesium / potassium and corrected/ionised calcium

investigation_details.investigation_name: Emergency electrolyte panel (calcium, magnesium, potassium, phosphate, corrected/ionised calcium)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The specific depletions cannot be quantified or safely replaced; delay risks seizure and arrhythmia.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The confirmatory test — obtainable only at ED, never in telehealth; the sealed answer is 'decreased serum cations'.

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG / QT interval and continuous cardiac monitoring

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypocalcaemia/hypomagnesaemia/hypokalaemia prolong the QT and risk ventricular arrhythmia — hospital monitoring, never available in telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Carpopedal spasm + perioral numbness + facial twitching + confusion together indicate a critical, deteriorating calcium (and mixed cation) depletion requiring emergency repletion.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency serum electrolytes (calcium, magnesium, potassium, phosphate, corrected/ionised calcium)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies the depletions to guide IV repletion; must be done urgently at ED, not achievable in telehealth.
Detects QT prolongation and arrhythmia risk from combined hypocalcaemia/hypomagnesaemia/hypokalaemia.
